cover.html:
- Welcome — Search-led Cisco workshop: AI Search, Federated Sources, Agent Builder.
- One Serverless Search project only — no O11Y/Security for this motion.
- Lab ready in ~3–4 minutes while we walk the deck.
agenda.html:
- Three modules, same indices end-to-end.
- Module 1 proves hybrid retrieval; 2 federates; 3 acts with agents.
- Keep the GTM frame: Search consolidation, not a multi-SKU sell.
pain.html:
- Pain is swivel-chair: Meraki → IOS-XE → DNA → wiki.
- Elastic meets engineers where they search.
- Target: one Search surface, shorter MTTR.
serverless.html:
- Why Serverless Search: speed, GTM fit, ES|QL skill reuse, workshop economics.
- Explicitly call out no Observability/Security requirement.
ai-search.html:
- Hybrid is the product story: BM25 + semantic + RRF.
- Buyer quote: one place for IOS-XE and Meraki docs.
hybrid.html:
- Walk the diagram left to right. Pause on RRF merge.
- Index name cisco-network-kb is what they will verify in the lab.
federated.html:
- Federate don't rip-and-replace — DCOS language.
- Sources stay; Elastic is the unified query layer.
esql.html:
- Same ES|QL for humans and agent tools.
- Branch 4471 is the recurring story thread into Module 3.
agents.html:
- Grounded tools on Search indices — no hallucinated runbooks.
- Still Search-only; no Observability SKU.
noc-story.html:
- 90-second exec close: Search → Federate → Agent.
- Practice this out loud before Module 3.
future.html:
- Clear bridge: today = managed + federated indices; roadmap = blob prefixes.
- Economic punch: don't pay twice for cold/archive.
architecture.html:
- Sources → federation layer → hot/cold tiers.
- Same tooling surface as the workshop (ES|QL, Enterprise Search, agents).
outcomes.html:
- Unified search, easy admin, TCO, bridge to lab.
- Leave analysts with 'one query across tiers'.
lab.html:
- Orient learners: es3-api, port 8080, four seed indices.
- When Kibana opens, start Module 1.
close.html: - Five takeaways — leave these on screen for questions.
thank-you.html: - Point to Instruqt manage URL and Pages deck for follow-up.
